titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to Katie Mooney, 19 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Peter Mooney writing to his sister to thank her for the things she has sent him and to ask about the family and farm.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Nov 19th 1915 Dear Katie, I received your letters
this morning alright. I
got the boots & tobacco
alright. The pipe was
just the sort I wanted.
I am sure I am giving
you all a lot of trouble
with all you have to
send me
I did not get the Numerals
I must have thrown
them away in the packing
as I got the Shino
alright. The next time
you go to Dublin you
can send them on.
You might send me on
another inside shirt.
I the one I have is
in flitters. I am doing
splendid here. The eleven
of us that came here
from Athlone are called
the Athlone drivers. The weather here is
grand now. Tell Tommy
not to delay in putting
on the slates. Tell
James to tell Mr Martin
about the slates in
Holiestown. Tommy can
settel them. I am glad
that Gray horse is jumping
so well tell Charley
to be careful of him
As regards coming here
to see me it would be
useless for you to come
here as I would have
no time to see you &
Christmas is not so
far off when I hope to
get home. You might
not send on the three
papers every day. The Independent
would be enough. You may
send on the Drogheda and Sport.
I have no more to say
at present so I say Good Bye
&God Bless you all. Peter Mooney
Miss K Mooney
Caulstown
Dunboyne
Co. Meath
